user_outcome: A SwornAgent operator selects an Ollama Cloud or supported local provider prefix, optionally sets providers.<prefix>.base_url in config.json, and the sworn verify entry point reaches the expected endpoint through one declared provider table without leaking credentials to keyless services.
rationale: This is the endpoint foundation for sworn#15. It replaces repeated OAI-compatible factory arms with explicit data while retaining inspectable prefix resolution. The table covers existing chat/completions providers and the new cloud/local providers; native and Responses drivers remain explicit because their wire formats genuinely differ. Config endpoint overrides become durable and visible, while the obsolete SWORN_<PROVIDER>_BASE_URL path is warned about and ignored. The slice owns contracts C-01, C-02, C-04, and C-05.
in_scope: Create a declared OAI chat endpoint descriptor table whose generic constructor supplies prefix, default base URL, credential provider, and existing structured mode; retain openai/ Responses, native anthropic/google/vertex/bedrock/azure/oci, claude-cli/, and ollama-native/ as explicit non-table dispatch paths.

Migrate existing OAI chat prefixes into the table without behavioural drift: openai-completions, deepseek, groq, mistral, openrouter, xai, cloudflare, and github retain their current default URL, credential, full model-subpath, and structured-mode semantics.

Add ollama-cloud/ (https://ollama.com/v1, credential provider ollama-cloud), ollama/ (http://localhost:11434/v1, keyless), llamacpp/ (http://localhost:8080/v1, keyless), lmstudio/ (http://localhost:1234/v1, keyless), vllm/ (http://localhost:8000/v1, keyless), and localai/ (http://localhost:8080/v1, keyless) as OAI chat descriptors with structured mode unsupported until S04/S05 discover and apply per-model capability.

Move the existing native Ollama /api/chat utility client to ollama-native/ with default http://localhost:11434; document and test that ollama/ now has one OAI-compatible meaning across utility and later loop dispatch paths.

Add Config.Providers as a map keyed by canonical prefix whose value is a provider object containing optional base_url; merge it into model.ProviderConfig through one cmd/sworn adapter used by verify now and the remaining commands in S02.

Validate config provider keys against the declared endpoint/native prefix set and base_url as an absolute http(s) URL with no userinfo, query, or fragment; reject unknown fields inside a provider object, and reject non-HTTPS overrides for descriptors that attach a credential. Permit zero or more trailing slashes, remove them during resolution, then append exactly one operation path beginning with `/`.

Add ollama-cloud -> OLLAMA_API_KEY to canonical credential resolution. Ensure ollama/, llamacpp/, lmstudio/, vllm/, and localai/ never attach any credential even when OLLAMA_API_KEY or another provider key is present.

Remove runtime use of SWORN_<PROVIDER>_BASE_URL. When one is set, emit a value-free warning naming the variable and providers.<prefix>.base_url, ignore it, and resolve from config then descriptor default. Extend sworn doctor with the same migration finding.
Preserve the no-key/no-payload logging contract and full-model-name pass-through for model IDs containing additional slashes.
out_of_scope: Loop registry registration and keyless /models availability probes are S02-keyless-loop-dispatch.
Dialect probing, generated dialect records, and OAI request adaptation are S03-endpoint-dialect-conformance.
Live workflow matrix and external provider evidence are S04-live-provider-matrix, except the opt-in owner tests required by contracts C-02/C-04/C-05.
Per-model capability discovery and role eligibility remain S04-provider-registry and S05-capability-eligibility in 2026-07-11-contract-edge-gates.
Adding credentials, capability claims, pricing data, models, or role selection inside config provider objects is forbidden.

effort_complexity.rationale: The implementation is a broad public-contract migration across config, credential, utility dispatch, doctor, and provider construction. The correct structure is known, but preserving every existing provider while changing Ollama prefix semantics and eliminating an override path creates a wide blast radius.

risks.description: The endpoint table becomes a second registry whose prefix, credential, or wire meaning drifts from loop dispatch.
risks.mitigation: S01 makes the table the construction authority consumed by model.NewClient; S02's registry derives its OAI prefix groups from declared descriptors and tests registry/NewClient agreement for every row. No second hand-written provider list is allowed.

risks.description: OLLAMA_API_KEY is accidentally attached to local ollama/ requests or a keyed endpoint is overridden to plaintext HTTP.
risks.mitigation: Credential attachment is descriptor-owned, local descriptors declare none, config validation rejects non-HTTPS overrides for credentialed descriptors, and transport tests assert Authorization absence/presence without reflecting the secret.

risks.description: Removing BASE_URL environment overrides silently routes an operator to a different endpoint.
risks.mitigation: Every removed variable produces a value-free runtime warning and a doctor finding with the exact replacement config path; tests set both env and config and prove config/default wins.

risks.description: The ollama/ migration makes utility and loop paths disagree or silently removes native Ollama access.
risks.mitigation: Command-level tests prove ollama/ uses /v1/chat/completions and ollama-native/ uses /api/chat, and provider/registry consistency tests prevent role-dependent prefix meaning.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN a table-driven command test makes two `sworn verify` invocations for each of ollama-cloud/org/family/model, ollama/org/family/model, llamacpp/org/family/model, lmstudio/org/family/model, vllm/org/family/model, and localai/org/family/model using a temporary config.json whose absolute base_url is `<server>/gateway/v1///` (TLS for ollama-cloud) THE SYSTEM SHALL remove all trailing slashes, POST exactly to `<server>/gateway/v1/chat/completions` with no query, and send model `org/family/model` verbatim on both invocations; the valid-verdict fixture SHALL return PASS with exit 0 and the failing-verdict fixture SHALL return FAIL with exit 1 (C-01, C-04, N-01).

THE SYSTEM SHALL construct one declared OAI endpoint table with these exact prefix/default-base/credential/structured-mode rows: openai-completions=https://api.openai.com/v1,openai,response_format; deepseek=https://api.deepseek.com/v1,deepseek,tool_call; groq=https://api.groq.com/openai/v1,groq,unsupported; mistral=https://api.mistral.ai/v1,mistral,unsupported; openrouter=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1,openrouter,unsupported; xai=https://api.x.ai/v1,xai,response_format; cloudflare=https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/ai/v1,cloudflare,unsupported; github=https://models.inference.ai.azure.com,github,unsupported; ollama-cloud=https://ollama.com/v1,ollama-cloud,unsupported; ollama=http://localhost:11434/v1,none,unsupported; llamacpp=http://localhost:8080/v1,none,unsupported; lmstudio=http://localhost:1234/v1,none,unsupported; vllm=http://localhost:8000/v1,none,unsupported; localai=http://localhost:8080/v1,none,unsupported. Every row SHALL preserve the model substring after the first slash. `TestEndpointTableIsPrefixAuthority` SHALL parse internal/model/provider.go with stdlib go/parser and prove NewClient contains one endpoint-table lookup/generic-constructor branch and no switch case for any listed prefix; the same test SHALL pass synthetic descriptor `fixture=https://fixture.invalid/v1,none,unsupported` and model `org/family/model` directly to the generic constructor and assert concrete type `*model.OAI`, BaseURL `https://fixture.invalid/v1`, Model `org/family/model`, empty APIKey, and StructuredUnsupported without editing any provider or credential switch (N-01).

WHEN model.NewClient or the `sworn verify` entry point resolves ollama/<model> THE SYSTEM SHALL use `*model.OAI` with default base http://localhost:11434/v1 and the verify transport SHALL observe POST /v1/chat/completions. WHEN either caller resolves ollama-native/<model> THE SYSTEM SHALL use the native Ollama client with default host http://localhost:11434 and the verify transport SHALL observe POST /api/chat. Loop registry and role agreement are excluded here and SHALL be proven by S02 (N-01).

FOR EACH JSON fixture containing an unknown provider key, an unknown provider-object field, or a relative/non-HTTP(S)/userinfo/query/fragment base_url, plus a plaintext HTTP base_url for a credentialed descriptor: `config.Load` reading that fixture SHALL return an error. FOR EACH equivalent invalid value representable by Config (unknown provider key and every invalid base_url category), `Config.Validate` SHALL return an error and `config.Save` SHALL return an error without creating or changing its target file; the unknown-field case is decode-only because Config has no field in which to retain it. `sworn verify` using every JSON fixture SHALL make zero transport requests, exit 2, and write stderr naming respectively `providers.<key>`, `providers.<prefix>.<field>`, or `providers.<prefix>.base_url` without printing any configured value or credential (C-01, N-01).

WHEN non-empty OLLAMA_API_KEY exists in the environment THE SYSTEM SHALL use that exact value ahead of credentials.json; WHEN the environment value is absent or empty it SHALL use a non-empty providers.ollama-cloud value from credentials.json. For ollama-cloud/ over HTTPS it SHALL send exactly `Authorization: Bearer <resolved-value>`. IF both sources are absent or empty THEN `sworn verify --verifier-model ollama-cloud/<model>` SHALL make zero transport requests, exit 2, and name OLLAMA_API_KEY plus the credentials path without printing a value. For ollama/, llamacpp/, lmstudio/, vllm/, and localai/ it SHALL omit Authorization even when either source contains a value (C-02, C-04, C-05, N-01).

WHEN one or more of SWORN_OPENAI_COMPLETIONS_BASE_URL, SWORN_DEEPSEEK_BASE_URL, SWORN_GROQ_BASE_URL, SWORN_MISTRAL_BASE_URL, SWORN_OPENROUTER_BASE_URL, SWORN_XAI_BASE_URL, SWORN_CLOUDFLARE_BASE_URL, or SWORN_GITHUB_BASE_URL has a non-empty value, `sworn verify` SHALL inspect all eight variables regardless of the selected model, ignore every non-empty value, emit exactly one stderr line per non-empty variable in the form `warning: <VARIABLE> is ignored; set providers.<prefix>.base_url in config.json`, and resolve the selected endpoint from config then its descriptor default. `sworn doctor` SHALL inspect all eight variables, emit exactly one stdout check `[WARN] removed provider endpoint override — <VARIABLE> is ignored; set providers.<prefix>.base_url in config.json` per non-empty variable, emit no additional generic migration-warning line, and exit 0 when no ERROR-level doctor check exists. An absent or present-but-empty variable SHALL emit no warning or finding; neither command SHALL display a non-empty variable value (N-01).

THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve the exact existing-provider endpoint, credential, structured-mode, and full-model-subpath mappings enumerated in AC-02 while changing only the ratified ollama/ meaning in AC-03; `go test ./internal/config/... ./internal/model/... ./cmd/sworn/...` and `go vet ./...` SHALL exit 0, and `gofmt -l .` SHALL exit 0 with empty stdout (N-01).
